skill.name: tailwind-design-system
skill.description: Build Tailwind CSS design systems with design tokens, component variants, responsive layouts, dark mode, and accessibility patterns.
skill.summary: Create consistent Tailwind CSS design systems and reusable UI component patterns.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak crypto, network access, browser storage, and a critical combined heuristic. Review found the command and weak-crypto hits are false positives from Markdown code fences, Tailwind color tokens, and UI examples; no prompt injection or exfiltration behavior was found. The only confirmed concerns are low-risk documentation links and a localStorage theme preference example.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported external command locations are Markdown fences or inline resource references. They present Tailwind, CSS, and React examples and do not instruct the agent to run shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matching lines are code block delimiters or Markdown resource paths. I found no shell command invocation or execution instruction in those locations.

low_findings.title: Tailwind Design Tokens Misclassified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The reported weak-crypto matches refer to HSL color values, destructive UI variant names, and Tailwind token examples. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic operation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are visibly Tailwind CSS tokens and component variant names. There is no cryptographic API, algorithm selection, or secret handling context.

low_findings.title: Theme Preference Stored in Browser Storage
low_findings.description: The playbook includes localStorage get and set examples for a theme preference. This is a legitimate UI pattern, but implementers should avoid storing sensitive data through the same pattern.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The browser storage access is confirmed, but it is limited to a theme value. The residual risk is low because no credentials or personal data are stored.

low_findings.title: External Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The playbook links to Tailwind CSS, CVA, shadcn/ui, and Radix documentation. These links are informational and do not create automatic network requests from the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded URLs are plain Markdown documentation links. They do not transmit local data or invoke network calls by themselves.

content.value_statement: Teams need consistent UI foundations that scale across products. This skill guides Tailwind CSS tokens, component variants, responsive patterns, and accessible theming.
actual_capabilities: Defines Tailwind CSS semantic tokens for color, radius, spacing, and dark mode.
Shows component variant patterns using class-variance-authority and reusable utilities.
Provides examples for buttons, cards, inputs, grids, dialogs, and theme toggles.
Includes accessibility patterns for focus states, labels, ARIA attributes, and keyboard support.
Guides responsive layout decisions with container and grid component patterns.
Documents best practices for avoiding hardcoded colors and arbitrary utility sprawl.
limitations: It is guidance and examples, not a complete installable component package.
Examples assume React, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS familiarity.
Project-specific brand tokens and visual QA still require human decisions.
It does not audit every generated component for accessibility compliance.

use_cases.title: Create a shared component library
use_cases.description: Define reusable Tailwind components with variants, sizes, focus states, and consistent naming.

use_cases.title: Standardize product UI patterns
use_cases.description: Convert scattered utility classes into semantic tokens and repeatable layout patterns.

use_cases.title: Add theme support to an app
use_cases.description: Implement light, dark, and system theme behavior with CSS variables and Tailwind configuration.

prompt_templates.title: Define basic tokens
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new Tailwind project.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Tailwind design token plan for my app. Include semantic colors, radius values, spacing rules, and dark mode considerations.

prompt_templates.title: Build a component variant
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a reusable Tailwind button component with variants, sizes, disabled states, focus states, and accessibility notes.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor inconsistent UI
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these Tailwind class patterns and propose a design-system refactor using semantic tokens and reusable components.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a full system migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a migration plan from ad hoc Tailwind usage to a governed design system with tokens, components, documentation, and verification steps.

output_examples.input: A team needs consistent buttons, cards, and inputs across a React app.
output_examples.output: A component plan with shared variants, size rules, focus behavior, error states, and naming conventions.

output_examples.input: An app needs light and dark themes without hardcoded colors.
output_examples.output: A token structure using CSS variables, semantic color names, Tailwind configuration updates, and theme testing steps.

output_examples.input: A product grid breaks across mobile and desktop layouts.
output_examples.output: A responsive grid pattern with column rules, container sizes, spacing guidance, and accessibility checks.

best_practices: Start with semantic tokens before writing component-specific classes.
Document component variants, sizes, states, and accessibility expectations together.
Test light mode, dark mode, keyboard navigation, and responsive breakpoints before release.
anti_patterns: Do not copy many arbitrary values across components instead of extending the theme.
Do not store sensitive data in browser storage examples adapted from theme code.
Do not skip focus states, labels, or error descriptions in form components.

faq.question: Does this skill generate a full component library?
faq.answer: It provides patterns and examples that help build one. It is not a packaged library by itself.

faq.question: Which frameworks does it assume?
faq.answer: The examples focus on Tailwind CSS with React and TypeScript. Many token ideas apply to other stacks.

faq.question: Can it help with dark mode?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes CSS variable and theme provider patterns for light, dark, and system themes.

faq.question: Does it cover accessibility?
faq.answer: Yes. It highlights focus rings, labels, ARIA attributes, error states, and keyboard-friendly component behavior.

faq.question: Is it safe to use in Claude, Codex, or Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes, with normal review. The audit found low-risk documentation examples, not malicious behavior.

faq.question: What should I provide when using it?
faq.answer: Provide your framework, brand tokens, component scope, accessibility requirements, and examples of current UI problems.
